login.title: Sign in to PADIS
login.description: Use your account to access the dashboard, download CSV files, and use PADIS report features.
login.privacyNotice: PADIS only stores authentication tokens in your browser — no tracking or analytics.
login.checkingSessionDesc: PADIS is verifying your authentication status.
login.heroDesc: Login to explore risk analysis results, view interactive maps, compare projection and baseline scenarios, and download PADIS outputs.
login.webgisDesc: Spatial visualization of loss and AAL per district.
login.accessDesc: Login system to control data, report, and admin features.
login.errOauthFailed: Google login failed or was cancelled. Please try again.
login.errOauthBridgeFailed: Google login succeeded but failed to create a PADIS session. Please try again.
login.learnMoreText: Want to learn more about PADIS?

register.title: Create a PADIS account
register.description: Complete the form below to create a new account and start accessing the PADIS platform.
register.heroTitle: Create an account to access PADIS features
register.heroDesc: Register your account to access the risk dashboard, spatial visualizations, projection and baseline comparisons, and download features.
register.dashboardDesc: Explore risk maps, statistics, and integrated analytics charts.
register.accessDesc: User accounts can be managed to support PADIS feature access control.
register.noteDesc: After successful registration, the account can be used immediately or may require admin activation depending on PADIS system configuration.
register.successMessage: Registration successful. Please login with your new account.
register.errPasswordTooShort: Password must be at least 8 characters.
register.errPasswordMismatch: Password and confirmation do not match.

forgotPassword.title: Forgot your password?
forgotPassword.description: Enter your PADIS account email. If the email is registered, the system will send a password reset link to your inbox.
forgotPassword.heroDesc: Enter your account email to receive a password reset link. You can then set a new password and regain access to the PADIS dashboard.
forgotPassword.securityDesc: Reset tokens are created with a 30-minute validity to keep your account secure.
forgotPassword.emailSentDesc: The reset link is sent directly to your registered email and never displayed in the browser.
forgotPassword.sentDesc: A password reset link has been sent to:
forgotPassword.sentStep1: Open your email app or gmail.com
forgotPassword.sentStep2: Look for an email from "PADIS" with subject "Reset Password Akun PADIS"
forgotPassword.sentStep3: Click the Reset Password button in the email
forgotPassword.sentStep4: Create a new password and log back in
forgotPassword.expiry: The link is valid for 30 minutes. Also check your Spam / Junk folder if the email doesn't appear.

resetPassword.title: Set new password
resetPassword.titleFull: Set a new password for your PADIS account
resetPassword.description: Use this page to complete the account recovery process. After updating your password, you can log back into PADIS with your new credentials.
resetPassword.securityDesc: The reset link is time-limited and will be deactivated after the password is updated.
resetPassword.accessDesc: After success, you can return to accessing the PADIS dashboard and analysis features.
resetPassword.successDesc: Your new password has been saved. You will be redirected to the login page automatically.
resetPassword.tipsDesc: Use at least 8 characters with a combination of uppercase, lowercase, numbers, and special characters.
resetPassword.noToken: Reset token not found. Please repeat the forgot password process.
resetPassword.noTokenDesc: Make sure you opened this page from the reset link sent by the system to your email.
resetPassword.errTokenNotFound: Password reset token not found or invalid.
resetPassword.errPasswordTooShort: New password must be at least 8 characters.
resetPassword.errPasswordMismatch: Password and confirmation do not match.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.riskMapDesc: Explore the distribution of direct losses and AAL per region based on active analysis parameters.
dashboard.statisticsDesc: AAL comparison across hazard types, total direct losses for projection and baseline scenarios, top affected regions, and loss distribution.
dashboard.noDataFilter: No data for this filter combination — try changing the hazard type or return period
dashboard.loginRequiredMsg: Please login first to download CSV or generate reports.
dashboard.sessionExpired: Your session has expired. Please login again.
dashboard.errServerAnalysis: Failed to connect to analysis server. Try reloading the page.
dashboard.loadingDesc: Please wait, the system is preparing the visualization.
dashboard.selectRegion: Select a region on the map or dropdown
dashboard.activateLayer: Activate a layer to view regional data.
dashboard.hazardDisabledMsg: Hazard Index is not available for Multi-hazard as it is calculated from the combination of drought and flood.
dashboard.mapVisualizationDesc: Click a map area to see details and regional indicators.
dashboard.loginForReport: Please login first to download the PDF report.

map.hazardDisabled: Hazard Index is not available for Multi-hazard as it is calculated from the combination of drought and flood.

landing.badge: WebGIS PADIS: Paddy Disaster Information System
landing.heroTitle1: Rice Disaster Risk Analysis Based on
landing.heroDesc: PADIS helps understand spatial rice losses through flood, drought, and multi-hazard analysis, with projection and baseline comparisons to support data-driven decision making.
landing.systemSummaryDesc: PADIS integrates disaster data, projection and baseline scenarios, and rice production data to generate direct loss estimates and Average Annual Loss (AAL) spatially.
landing.coverageDesc: PADIS integrates various models and data sources to produce more comprehensive risk analysis.
landing.hazardDesc: Uses HEC-RAS 2D for inundation simulation based on return periods of 25–250 years.
landing.droughtDesc: Uses SPI based on GPM and MME rainfall data for dry condition analysis.
landing.multiDesc: Integration of flood and drought models to generate combined risk analysis.
landing.projectionDesc: Analysis based on observational and projection data for various return periods.
landing.resultsDesc: PADIS presents risk outputs that help interpret spatial rice loss patterns.
landing.directLossDesc: Overview of direct loss and Average Annual Loss estimates.
landing.distributionDesc: Identify priority regions based on spatial risk.
landing.ctaDesc: Analyze losses, compare scenarios, and identify priority regions faster through the PADIS dashboard.
landing.photoCredit: Photo by Dhenny Napitupulu on Pexels

about.description: A Web-GIS for estimating national losses from reduced total rice production caused by flood, drought, and multi-hazard disasters.
about.hazardDesc: PADIS processes flood, drought, and multi-hazard information as the basis for rice field risk analysis.
about.lossDesc: The system connects hazard, climate conditions, and rice data to produce more measurable loss information.
about.vizDesc: Analysis results are displayed through WebGIS for easier understanding in evaluation and decision making.
about.techDesc: WebGIS on frontend, REST API and tile service on backend, and Python geospatial pipeline for risk analysis.
about.inputDesc: Hazard rasters, administrative boundaries, rice field areas, and production data.
about.analysisDesc: Reproject, overlay, zonal statistics, loss, AAL, and multi-hazard.
about.dataDesc: REST API, PostGIS, MVT tiles, lightweight GeoJSON, and report endpoints.
about.explorationDesc: WebGIS, charts, analysis filters, CSV/XLSX downloads, and report previews.
about.webgisDesc: Dashboard built as a Next.js and React application with filter controls, authentication, and responsive analytics views.
about.mapDesc: Interactive map uses Leaflet and vector tiles so loss, AAL, hazard, and production layers load efficiently.
about.apiDesc: Flask backend provides analytics, layer, tile, report, admin endpoints, and token-based authentication.
about.dbDesc: Run, region, loss, AAL, hazard, and production data connected to PostgreSQL/PostGIS via a Python database layer.
about.pipelineDesc: Preprocess, zonal statistics, loss, AAL, and multi-hazard processed with Python geospatial libraries.
about.vizOutDesc: Dashboard and analysis outputs use interactive charts, spreadsheet exports, and a report generator.
about.faculty: Faculty of Earth Sciences and Technology · Institut Teknologi Bandung
about.partnersDesc: PADIS was developed in cross-institutional collaboration supporting risk analysis, data, and decision making.
about.hopeDesc: PADIS is here to support structured, spatially-driven rice risk analysis.

caraKerja.title: From Spatial Data to Loss Estimates
caraKerja.description: Five spatial analysis stages that transform flood and drought raster data into direct loss estimates and Average Annual Loss at district level.
caraKerja.step1Desc: Flood and drought raster data are used as the hazard basis for various scenarios and return periods.
caraKerja.step2Desc: Hazard values are extracted from the rice field layer and aggregated to district level using spatial analysis.
caraKerja.step3Desc: Hazard values are transformed into Loss of Productivity (LOP): an indicator of potential impact on rice.
caraKerja.step4Desc: Loss of Productivity (LOP) is combined with total production data and Dry Unhusked Rice Price (GKP) to generate loss estimates in rupiah.
caraKerja.step5Desc: Direct loss estimates are aggregated into Average Annual Loss (AAL) and presented as interactive layers on the dashboard.
caraKerja.videoDesc: Watch a brief guide to understand the analysis workflow, output types, and how to read results on the dashboard.
caraKerja.flowDesc: PADIS processes hazard data into loss estimates and dashboard outputs through structured analysis stages.
caraKerja.dashComponentsDesc: The dashboard is designed to help users quickly understand rice risk through maps, filters, and interconnected analysis summaries.
caraKerja.compLayerDesc: Select hazard, scenario, and return period to update the map view.
caraKerja.compSearchDesc: Find specific districts and navigate directly to their location on the map.
caraKerja.compSummaryDesc: View direct loss and AAL values for the selected region in a concise summary panel.
caraKerja.compChartDesc: Explore risk distribution and comparisons across scenarios and return periods through charts.
caraKerja.compExportDesc: Download direct loss and AAL reports in CSV, XLSX, and PDF formats for further analysis.
caraKerja.useCaseDesc: How PADIS can support rice risk-based analysis and decision making.
caraKerja.useCase1Desc: Read rice loss patterns per district for flood, drought, and multi-hazard.
caraKerja.useCase2Desc: Compare projection and baseline conditions to systematically view risk changes.
caraKerja.useCase3Desc: Determine priority regions based on direct losses and Average Annual Loss (AAL).
caraKerja.ctaDesc: Use the PADIS dashboard to view loss estimates, compare scenarios, and spatially identify priority regions.
caraKerja.summaryText: In short, PADIS transforms hazard data into risk indicators, direct loss estimates, and Average Annual Loss (AAL), then presents them through an interactive dashboard.
caraKerja.dashboardIntegrationText: The PADIS dashboard integrates interactive maps, information panels, and analytics charts in one view, so users can read risk patterns and compare scenarios without losing context.
caraKerja.openYoutubeAria: Open PADIS guide video on YouTube

metodologi.title: Spatial Risk Analysis Methodology
metodologi.description: A raster-based system integrating hazard, vulnerability, and exposure to generate direct loss estimates and AAL at district level across Indonesia.
metodologi.historicalTitle: Annual Historical Disaster Trends (Indonesia Disaster Data by BNPB)
metodologi.weightingDesc: The following weights are used as the basis for multi-hazard composition based on historical occurrence and economic impact level.
metodologi.floodWeightDesc: This value represents the relative contribution of flood in the multi-hazard analysis.
metodologi.droughtWeightDesc: This value represents the relative contribution of drought in the multi-hazard analysis composition.
metodologi.vulnDesc: The vulnerability curve represents the relationship between the disaster index and the level of rice loss of productivity for each hazard.
metodologi.metadataDesc: Catalog of main data sources and technical parameters used as inputs for risk modeling.
metodologi.ctaDesc: Use the PADIS dashboard to view loss estimates, compare scenarios, and spatially identify priority regions.
metodologi.adminBoundaryDesc: Used as the primary spatial reference for spatial joins and aggregation of direct loss & AAL estimates to district level.
metodologi.riceCoverDesc: Spatial layer representing rice field land cover areas. Used for masking and extracting hazard values via spatial overlay.
metodologi.floodModelingDesc: Raster layer from 2D hydrodynamic simulation using HEC-RAS 2D Analysis containing inundation depth values in meters for integration with vulnerability curves.
metodologi.droughtIndexDesc: Raster layer of Standardized Precipitation Index values representing extreme rainfall deficit based on satellite observations and Multi-Model Ensemble projections.
metodologi.justificationText: PADIS modeling is based on a comparison between the historically high frequency of disaster events (Hazard) and areas with the highest rice production levels (Exposure).
metodologi.baselineDesc: Represents the hazard condition built using standard modeling parameters such as physical area data, spatial characteristics, and hydrological conditions. This scenario is not associated with future scenarios like RCP or SSP, but reflects conditions based on parameters commonly used in hazard model development.
metodologi.projectionDesc: Represents the hazard condition influenced by future scenarios. Projection can use two approaches: (1) RCP (Representative Concentration Pathways), based on climate projections such as changes in rainfall, temperature, and evapotranspiration; or (2) SSP (Shared Socioeconomic Pathways), based on socioeconomic projections such as land use changes, population growth, and regional infrastructure.
metodologi.processExplanation: Both scenarios are processed through the same risk calculation workflow: hazard value extraction, vulnerability curve application, direct loss estimation, and aggregation to Annual Average Loss (AAL) at district level.
metodologi.floodCurveDesc: This curve shows the relationship between flood inundation depth and rice loss of productivity. The formulation follows Hendrawan & Komori (2021) to represent flood vulnerability response in PADIS analysis.
metodologi.floodFormulaExplain: In this formulation, y represents the loss of productivity value, while x represents the maximum flood inundation depth (meters).
metodologi.droughtCurveDesc: This curve shows the relationship between drought intensity and rice loss of productivity. The formulation follows an adaptation of the drought vulnerability curve from Guo et al. (2021) for PADIS analysis.
metodologi.droughtFormulaExplain: In this formulation, y represents the loss of productivity value, while x represents the normalized drought index.
metodologi.scenariosDescPart2: refer to two scenarios for constructing
metodologi.scenariosDescPart3: . Both use the same calculation workflow, but differ in the

kebijakanPrivasi.title: PADIS Privacy Policy
kebijakanPrivasi.description: Data processed by PADIS, reasons for processing, third-party services, and how users manage their account data.
kebijakanPrivasi.lastUpdated: Last updated: May 2026 · ITB academic project
kebijakanPrivasi.summaryText: PADIS does not sell user data, does not display ads, and does not use third-party analytics trackers. Account data is used only for authentication, feature access, and system security.
kebijakanPrivasi.dataProcessedSubtitle: Data is collected as necessary to run accounts and PADIS features.
kebijakanPrivasi.dataProcessedDesc: PADIS processes account data, authentication tokens, and security records needed for the dashboard, admin panel, and password reset to function correctly.
kebijakanPrivasi.accountDesc: Name, email address, account role, account status, and last login time to manage user and administrator access.
kebijakanPrivasi.credentialsDesc: Passwords are stored as hashes, not plaintext. JWT authentication tokens are stored in browser localStorage to keep the session active.
kebijakanPrivasi.resetDesc: If a user requests a password reset, the system stores the reset token hash, expiry time, and token usage status.
kebijakanPrivasi.auditDesc: Certain administrative actions are logged for accountability, including user changes and password reset completions.
kebijakanPrivasi.purpose1: Recognize logged-in users and keep their session active.
kebijakanPrivasi.purpose2: Determine access rights between regular users and administrators.
kebijakanPrivasi.purpose3: Manage password resets securely through single-use tokens.
kebijakanPrivasi.purpose4: Provide an audit trail for sensitive admin actions.
kebijakanPrivasi.notDone1: Does not sell, rent, or trade user data.
kebijakanPrivasi.notDone2: Does not display ads, marketing pixels, Hotjar, or similar trackers.
kebijakanPrivasi.notDone3: Does not store original passwords in readable plaintext.
kebijakanPrivasi.notDone4: Does not use map activity data for commercial profiling.
kebijakanPrivasi.thirdPartySubtitle: External infrastructure for hosting, authentication, email, and base maps.
kebijakanPrivasi.supabaseDesc: Used for Google login and OAuth identity verification. Data received by PADIS is primarily name and email.
kebijakanPrivasi.hostingDesc: Used for hosting the frontend, backend API, and application database and analysis data storage.
kebijakanPrivasi.smtpDesc: Used to send password reset links to the user's registered email address.
kebijakanPrivasi.basemapDesc: Used as base maps. PADIS does not send name, email, or tokens to basemap providers, but tile requests may include technical data such as IP address and user-agent.
kebijakanPrivasi.rightsSubtitle: Users can request access, correction, or deletion of their account data.
kebijakanPrivasi.rightsDesc: Requests can be submitted through the PADIS developer contact. Some technical data may be retained temporarily if required for security, audit, or system integrity.
kebijakanPrivasi.right1: Request access to stored account data.
kebijakanPrivasi.right2: Request correction of inaccurate name, email, status, or other account information.
kebijakanPrivasi.right3: Request deletion of account and related authentication data.
kebijakanPrivasi.right4: Sign out of active sessions by clearing local tokens via the Sign Out button.
kebijakanPrivasi.retentionDesc: Account data is stored as long as the account is needed. Password reset tokens have a limited validity period, while audit logs are stored for system accountability.
kebijakanPrivasi.securityDesc: PADIS uses password hashing, authentication tokens, role-based access control, rate limiting for sensitive endpoints, and HTTPS on public deployments.
kebijakanPrivasi.academicDesc: PADIS is a Capstone academic project, not a commercial product. Account data is processed to run system functions, not for monetization.
kebijakanPrivasi.contactSubtitle: Privacy inquiries can be directed to the PADIS development team.
kebijakanPrivasi.contactDesc: This page is compiled for the transparency of the PADIS academic project and observes the general principles of personal data protection in Indonesia, including Law No. 27 of 2022 on Personal Data Protection.

report.topRegions: Top Three Highest Risk Priority Regions
report.aalComparisonTitle: B. AAL Comparison: Baseline vs Projection
report.allDetailsNote: Note: Complete regional data can be found in the Excel attachment (.xlsx).
report.lossDefinition: Estimated rice production loss due to a disaster at a given return period, expressed in Indonesian Rupiah.
report.aalDefinition: Average expected annual loss, calculated from the integral of the hazard probability curve × vulnerability curve.
report.hazardIndexDef: Normalized value (0–1) of spatial hazard intensity per region based on raster data.
report.returnPeriodDef: Average return period — probability that a disaster of that intensity or greater occurs once in RP years.
report.hazardDataDesc: High-resolution hazard rasters from hydrological models (flood) and climatological models (drought).
report.exposureDataDesc: District rice production (BPS) and district-level administrative boundaries.
report.vulnerabilityDesc: Loss function curves based on scientific references and historical disaster validation.
report.spatialIntegrationDesc: Zonal statistics via raster–vector overlay using Python GIS pipeline (geopandas/rasterio).
report.systemDesc: Agricultural Disaster Risk Analysis System Based on GIS
report.previewDesc: A4 layout preview — use the "Download PDF" button to download
report.excelTooltip: Download structured data (.xlsx) — 3 sheets: Summary, Top 10 Regions, All Data.
report.pdfHowToDesc: Click "Download PDF" → in the browser print dialog select Destination: Save as PDF → Save · Optimized for A4 portrait
